headlines.title: Iran's intelligence minister Esmail Khatib killed in air strike
headlines.description: Israel said it had killed Khatib, whose death was confirmed by Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian on Wednesday.

headlines.title: Israel destroys river bridges in southern Lebanon
headlines.description: The health ministry says that 968 people, including more than 100 children, have been killed since 2 March.

headlines.title: One killed as ski gondola crashes down mountain at Swiss resort
headlines.description: Dramatic footage showed the gondola flipping over several times in the snow before coming to a halt in Engelberg.

headlines.title: Oil nears $110 a barrel after gas field strike
headlines.description: Iran's military warned it would take "decisive action" in response to the strike on its energy infrastructure.

headlines.title: Hittler faces Zielinski as French town's election contest goes viral
headlines.description: Charles Hittler, mayor of Arcis-sur-Aube, has always faced jokes about his name but now he faces a rival with another unlikely name.

headlines.title: Forty years after the last one was poached, rhinos are back in the wild in Uganda
headlines.description: The return of southern white rhinos from the country comes after Ugandan poachers once wiped them out.

headlines.title: Russian archaeologist can be sent to Ukraine for trial, Polish judge rules
headlines.description: Archaeologist Alexander Butyagin is accused of conducting illegal excavations in Crimea - Ukraine's peninsula annexed by Russia.

headlines.title: Norway prosecutor asks for seven years' jail for crown princess's son in rape trial
headlines.description: Prosecutors in the trial of Marius Borg Høiby argue he should be found guilty of 39 of the 40 charges he is accused of.

headlines.title: US judge orders Trump administration to reopen Voice of America
headlines.description: Some 85% of VOA staff were laid off by Trump who accused the international broadcaster of bias.

headlines.title: King praises 'living bridge' with Nigeria at glitzy banquet
headlines.description: At the state banquet in Windsor Castle, King Charles praises the UK's partnership with Nigeria.

headlines.title: Chinese national charged for trying to smuggle 2,000 ants from Kenya
headlines.description: Zhang Kequn was found with the large consignment of ants in his luggage at a Nairobi airport.

headlines.title: Suspected stray bullet from military drill hits girl at playground
headlines.description: South Korea's military has since halted all shooting drills involving small firearms.

headlines.title: Air strike hit Kabul rehab centre as patients ate dinner, survivor tells BBC
headlines.description: The exact number killed in Monday night's strike by Pakistan is still not known, but is feared to be in the hundreds.

headlines.title: What have US allies and China said about sending ships to the Strait of Hormuz?
headlines.description: US allies have either pushed back or remained cautious on Trump's initial request to send ships to the crucial oil route.

headlines.title: India's cheap weight-loss drugs could reshape global obesity fight
headlines.description: With India's semaglutide patent expiring on 20 March, about 50 branded generics are expected to enter the market.

headlines.title: Australia shock jock Kyle Sandilands sacked and top-rating show cancelled
headlines.description: It comes after an on-air spat between Sandilands and his co-host Jackie 'O' Henderson a month ago.

headlines.title: Kerr winner sends Australia into Asian Cup final
headlines.description: Captain Sam Kerr scores the winner as Australia beat China 2-1 to reach the final of the Asian Cup.

headlines.title: Family of alleged Bondi gunmen have received death threats, court hears
headlines.description: Naveed Akram is seeking a court order to suppress his family's identity over fears for their safety.

headlines.title: Sri Lanka declares Wednesdays off as Asian countries try to conserve fuel
headlines.description: This is the latest in a series of measures undertaken by Asian countries which rely on oil from the Gulf.

headlines.title: Trump seeks to delay meeting with Xi in China
headlines.description: The escalating war in Iran has overshadowed many of the US' foreign policy objectives

headlines.title: All doctors in England warned to watch for meningitis symptoms after Kent outbreak
headlines.description: Health workers across England are urged to look out for signs of infection as thousands have jabs.

headlines.title: PM swerves questions on whether he spoke to Mandelson over Epstein friendship
headlines.description: Conservative leader Kemi Badenoch repeatedly asks if the prime minister spoke to Peter Mandelson about his friendship with Jeffrey Epstein before picking him to be a UK ambassador.

headlines.title: Extremist preacher Anjem Choudary loses challenge to overturn life sentence
headlines.description: The ruling means Choudary - considered by security chiefs to be one of the most dangerous men in Britain - cannot leave jail before he is 85.

headlines.title: Fly tippers in England face clearing up own rubbish as punishment
headlines.description: Fly tippers could face up to 20 hours of community service removing rubbish they have illegally dumped.

headlines.title: Cost of online passport application could rise to over £100
headlines.description: The Home Office says the increase will help it "move to a system that meets its costs through those who use it" and rely less on taxpayers.

headlines.title: Nursery employee worked with children after arrest
headlines.description: Kristian Parry, 26, found work at another nursery despite being arrested for making indecent images.

headlines.title: UK temperature reaches 21C in warmest day of year so far
headlines.description: The UK has seen its warmest day of the year so far with temperatures reaching 21C.

headlines.title: Police deny killer was charged to 'shut down stories'
headlines.description: Retired Det Supt Leigh Sanders has told a public inquiry that Valdo Calocane was charged early.

headlines.title: Body of 16-year-old found after car entered river
headlines.description: Searches continue for a missing male teenager who remains unaccounted for.

headlines.title: Average age of first time buyer in England climbs to 34
headlines.description: A new report looks at how conditions have changed for first time buyers since the 1990s.

headlines.title: 日米首脳会談 日本時間 20 日未明に イラン情勢などで意見交換へ
headlines.description: 高市総理大臣はアメリカのトランプ大統領との日米首脳会談に臨むため 18 日夜、日本を出発しました。会談は日本時間の 20 日未明に行われ、緊迫するイラン情勢への対応や、アメリカへの投資を含む経済協力をはじめ幅広い分野での連携強化などをめぐって意見を交わすことにしています。

headlines.title: 米 FRB 2 会合連続で利下げを見送り インフレ再び加速を警戒か
headlines.description: アメリカの FRB＝連邦準備制度理事会は金融政策を決める会合を開き、18 日、政策金利を据え置くことを決めたと発表しました。利下げの見送りは 2 会合連続で、パウエル議長は「中東情勢がアメリカ経済に及ぼす影響は不透明だ」と指摘し、今後の動向を注視する考えを示しました。

headlines.title: ガソリン小売価格 44 の都道府県で過去最高に【一覧掲載】
headlines.description: レギュラーガソリンの小売価格は、今月 16 日時点の全国平均で 1 リットルあたり 190.8 円と、イラン情勢を背景とした原油価格高騰の影響で過去最高になりました。これについて都道府県別に分析したところ、各地で前の週と比べて 23.9 円から 32.1 円の値上がりとなり、44 の都道府県で過去最も高くなったことがわかりました。

headlines.title: 新年度予算案 与党 集中審議開催へ調整 野党 暫定予算案求める
headlines.description: 新年度予算案をめぐり、与党側は、年度末までの成立を目指す姿勢は維持したうえで、丁寧な審議を進めたいとして、野党側の求めに応じ集中審議を開催する方向で調整しています。一方、野党側は、十分な審議時間を確保しようとすると年度内の成立は難しいとして、暫定予算案を編成するよう求めています。

headlines.title: 富士通子会社 金型を無償で保管させたか 公取委が勧告へ
headlines.description: レジの決済端末などを製造する富士通の子会社が、部品の製造を委託する事業者に金型を無償で保管させていたとして、公正取引委員会が近く、保管費用の支払いなどを求める勧告を出す方針を固めたことが、関係者への取材で分かりました。

headlines.title: 数学者の広中平祐さん死去 94 歳 フィールズ賞を受賞
headlines.description: 「数学のノーベル賞」とも言われるフィールズ賞を受賞した数学者で、文化勲章受章者の広中平祐さんが亡くなりました。94 歳でした。

headlines.title: 春闘 大手で高水準回答 中小や非正規で働く人への波及が焦点に
headlines.description: ことしの春闘は、18 日の集中回答日に大手企業で高い水準の回答が相次ぎました。この流れを中小企業や非正規雇用で働く人まで波及させて、物価の上昇を上回る賃上げを実現できるかが焦点です。

cnn.message: CNN RSS feed fetch failed - attempting alternative sources
